label: Accessibility and Assisted Travel Policy – All Networks

metro.wheelchair_access: All metro stations are step-free accessible. Lifts and ramps are available at all stations.
metro.boarding_ramps: Portable boarding ramps are available at all metro stations. Request from platform staff.
metro.dedicated_spaces: Each metro carriage has at least one dedicated wheelchair space. These spaces must be vacated for wheelchair users on request.
metro.lift_outages: In the event of a lift outage, passengers should inform station staff who will arrange alternative assistance or route guidance.

national_rail.wheelchair_access: All national rail stations are wheelchair accessible. Ramps are available on request from platform staff.
national_rail.boarding_ramps: Ramp assistance is provided by platform staff. Passengers requiring ramp assistance should notify staff on arrival at the station.
national_rail.dedicated_spaces: All national rail trains have at least two dedicated wheelchair spaces, bookable in advance at no extra charge.

wheelchair_space_booking.how_to_book: Contact customer service at least 24 hours before travel, or book via the app where the option is available.
wheelchair_space_booking.note: Wheelchair spaces are subject to availability. Early booking is strongly recommended.

pre_booking.description: Passengers requiring boarding assistance should contact customer service at least 24 hours before travel.
pre_booking.contact_method: In-app chat, email, or station help desk.
pre_booking.note: Where possible, same-day assistance will be provided, but cannot be guaranteed without advance notice.

on_the_day.description: Passengers with mobility difficulties are welcome to board first. Please inform platform staff on arrival.

metro.guide_dogs: Guide dogs and registered assistance animals are permitted at all times on all metro services without a carrier and at no charge.
metro.other_animals: Only registered assistance animals are exempt from the standard pet carrier policy. See travel_policies.json for full pet rules.

national_rail.guide_dogs: Guide dogs and registered assistance animals are permitted at all times in all coaches at no charge.
national_rail.documentation: Staff may ask for evidence that an animal is a registered assistance animal. Passengers are encouraged to carry relevant documentation.

visual_and_hearing_impairments.audio_announcements: All metro and national rail services provide audio announcements of upcoming stations.
visual_and_hearing_impairments.visual_displays: All carriages and stations feature digital display boards showing next stops, service status, and disruption alerts.
visual_and_hearing_impairments.assistance_request: Passengers with visual or hearing impairments requiring additional support should contact customer service in advance or speak to station staff on arrival.

metro.description: Seats marked with a yellow priority sign are reserved for passengers with disabilities, elderly passengers (65+), pregnant women, and passengers travelling with young children.
metro.rule: All other passengers must vacate priority seats immediately when requested.

national_rail.description: All coaches include priority seats marked with a blue priority sign.
national_rail.rule: Priority seats are reserved for passengers with disabilities, elderly passengers (65+), pregnant women, and passengers travelling with young children. Other passengers must vacate on request.

complaint_and_feedback.description: Passengers who experienced difficulties with accessibility or assisted travel may submit feedback or a complaint via in-app chat, email, or at a station help desk.
complaint_and_feedback.escalation: Unresolved accessibility complaints may be escalated to the relevant national transport accessibility regulator.
